comment: This is generated by ESQL's AbstractFunctionTestCase. Do not edit it. See ../README.md for how to regenerate it.
name: tstep
description:
Creates groups of values - buckets - out of a `@timestamp` attribute.

TSTEP expects the first argument to be either bucket duration, or integer number of target buckets in a provided range.

The optional `from` and `to` time range is used to derive bucket duration if integer number of target buckets provided.
Unlike `TBUCKET`, that
aligns time grid at calendar boundaries, `TSTEP` aligns at the query range, so that the first bucket
is computed on the range from `from` to `from` + `step` and labeled after right boundary.

When using ES|QL in Kibana, the range can be derived automatically from the
`@timestamp` filter
that Kibana adds to the query.

params.name: step
params.description: Fixed bucket width on a UTC grid, or a target bucket count. When a bucket count is provided, the actual step width is derived from `from` and `to` by dividing the range into equal-width fixed intervals at millisecond precision. When `from` and `to` are omitted, the range is derived from the request `@timestamp` filter.

params.name: from
params.description: Start of the time range that anchors the step grid. Required together with `to`.

params.name: to
params.description: End of the time range. Required together with `from`.

params.name: step
params.description: Fixed bucket width on a UTC grid, or a target bucket count. When a bucket count is provided, the actual step width is derived from `from` and `to` by dividing the range into equal-width fixed intervals at millisecond precision. When `from` and `to` are omitted, the range is derived from the request `@timestamp` filter.

params.name: step
params.description: Fixed bucket width on a UTC grid, or a target bucket count. When a bucket count is provided, the actual step width is derived from `from` and `to` by dividing the range into equal-width fixed intervals at millisecond precision. When `from` and `to` are omitted, the range is derived from the request `@timestamp` filter.

params.name: from
params.description: Start of the time range that anchors the step grid. Required together with `to`.

params.name: to
params.description: End of the time range. Required together with `from`.

examples:
FROM sample_data
| STATS min = MIN(@timestamp), max = MAX(@timestamp) BY bucket = TSTEP(1 hour)
| SORT bucket

FROM sample_data
| STATS min = MIN(@timestamp), max = MAX(@timestamp) BY bucket = TSTEP(1 hour, "2023-10-23T12:15:00.000Z", "2023-10-23T13:55:01.543Z")
| SORT bucket

FROM sample_data
| STATS min = MIN(@timestamp), max = MAX(@timestamp) BY bucket = TSTEP(2, "2023-10-23T12:15:00.000Z", "2023-10-23T13:55:01.543Z")
| SORT bucket
